entry.title: Re: First steps to work with SAP Cloud ALM Deployment scenario for SAP ABAP systems (7.40 or higher)

entry.title: Re: A 25x faster mta.yaml build for SAP CAP Node.js projects
entry.content: Great tip, I used this in a project recently and worked like a charm. I’m wondering why all the official samples all use the npm builder.

entry.title: Re: How to bulk upload data to a custom business object
entry.content:
Hi yujitsumura

The OData service exposure in effect creates an available API. 

So you would need to then call the API from some tool or developed app.

For example, one way would be to create an iFlow using Cloud Integration suite

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/use-csv-to-xml-converter?locale=en-US

You cloud also create a new app with Developer Extensibility that allows mass upload.

Or you could test it with Postman, as mentioned in this Blog:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/part-viii-custom-business-object-exposure-as-external-web-service-bonus/ba-p/13321117

Here are a couple other links:
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/multiple-data-entry-in-custom-business-object-without-odata-service/qaq-p/12801300

As well as this tutorial:

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-custom-ui-business-object..html

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: CCM - use multiple condition tables within one condit...
entry.content:
Hi @SD_Jedi, 

Thanks for the detailed explanation—your scenario is standard and should work correctly with proper access sequence setup.

I recommend copying the standard access sequence RE01 and adding your custom condition table (Contract / Material Group). Make sure to activate the "Exclusive" indicator and assign Requirement 75 to the first step. This ensures the system stops searching once a condition is found for the material group and avoids summing multiple rebate percentages. For the fallback step (Contract only), leave the exclusive flag and requirement blank. This configuration should give you the expected behavior: 8% for material group A and 6% otherwise, without doubling values.

If you're still seeing incorrect results, check for any enhancements or custom logic that might be overriding standard pricing behavior.

Best regards,
Gauthier Victor

entry.title: SAP Community AI Conversation Chatbot to ask Q&A?
entry.content:
Do you have some place where we can ask Qs to get quick answers based tons of community questions been answered and accepted as solution... This will benefit for us instead of waiting for answer from community colleagues... SAP Community is having huge knowledge database which can be utilised in a better way so this will increase more non SAP colleagues to join SAP community to get more members .. Do you have vector db for us to use

Any APIs do you plan to release which we can build our own chatbot in Gradio or streamlit

entry.title: Quering document lines in user define values
entry.content:
Hello! Im trying to get the "Quantity" field on the document lines in a "User defined values" query. 

SELECT $\[$38.11.0]
FROM DUMMY

I've been trying to use the previous query, and after reading through other questions on the subject I've tried:
Removing the backslash
Leaving only the select line
Changing "$38" for "RDR1"
Changing "0" for "number"
Changing "11" for "Quantity"

None of these have worked for me so far, and it either gives me internal error 3006 or syntax error near "$"

I'd appreciate any help on the subject

Thanks!

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch München (06/2025)
entry.content:
Liebe Stammtischschwestern und -brüder,

Wir treffen uns am Donnerstag, den 12.06., zum SAP Stammtisch. Lasst uns in schönem gemütlichen Ambiente zum Thema SAP und drumherum austauschen.

Wir haben diesen Monat als Themenschwerpunkt die neue SAP Sales und Service Cloud v2 mit zwei Experten auf dem Gebiet:

  • Andreas Neumeier (SAP) mit einem kurzen Überblick über die Funktionalitäten
  • Johannes Engelke (Freelance) zum Vorgehen bei Erweiterungen mit side-by-side Extensions

Wir treffen uns TBA. Bitte haltet auch eure Zu- und Absage aktuell, damit wir die richtige Personenzahl für unsere Planung haben.

Wir freuen uns auf Euer kommen und einen regen Austausch!

SAP Stammtisch Orga Team

entry.title: How to use SAP BPA send Adaptive Cards to personal Teams chat?
entry.content:
Hey guys, 

I’m building a process in SAP Build Process Automation, and in the Approve step, I want to send the form as an Adaptive Card to Microsoft Teams so that the user can approve it directly in Teams. Is that possible?

If you know, can you please share the solution with me?

Thank in advance

Giang

entry.title: Re: Impact Analysis in Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi, Cihan,

I am seeing your old question here and see it was not answered, so I wanted to check in with you.

In case you haven't already taken these steps, I would advise that you log your idea in the Influence Campaign to share the thoughts of what you need to fulfill your ability to track changes in requirements....are you looking for counts in a report or access to know 'who' is changing them or what is being changed, maybe a History log, or ....something else?  

Whatever it is, consider your needs and log your request in the Influence Campaign:

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Operations https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3525

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Issues with Checkbox Binding in Chart of Accounts Fiori App
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m working on a customization of the Standard Fiori App: Chart of Accounts and I'm encountering an issue with the behavior of CheckBox controls in different views within an IconTabFilter.

The problem arises because one of the IconTabFilter items uses a Tree View (All accounts), while the other uses a List View(Unassigned Accounts)  Both views contain checkboxes that are bound to the selected property. The values are either "X" (which should be true) or an empty string "" (which should be false). but this is causing a UI bug for the user.

Error:
Error: "X" is of type string, expected boolean for property "selected" of Element sap.m.CheckBox#__box225-__clone1365
Error: "" is of type string, expected boolean for property "selected" of Element sap.m.CheckBox#__box225-__clone136

Additionally, I am seeing the following error in the Console Log:

Oops, the Odata service delivered data in wrong order!

entry.title: SAP WS&O BY CLICKSOFTWARE 2.3 patch 15 - URGENT REQUIRMENT
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We are in a situation with our SAP HANA update 1909. We are stuck with upgrade and there is no forward path due to the fact we are using SAP WS&O BY CLICKSOFTWARE 2.3 patch 14 which is not compatible with 2023 S4HANA and the product is no more supported by SAP. The patch 15 of SAP WS&O BY CLICKSOFTWARE 2.3 is compatible with SAP HANA 2023 but there is no way we can get the patch for this product. SAP or SalesForce both do not have the patch 15 and we are literally stuck. We are not able to uninstall this product and CLICK is heavily used by us and we have 2500 field user using it. We understood we need to look into other product like SAP FSM but for us to move forward with S4 HANA 2023 update we need patch 15 of SAP WS&O BY CLICKSOFTWARE 2.3.

Does anyone has patch 15 of this product SAP WS&O BY CLICKSOFTWARE 2.3 and if you can find or know someone who can give use the patch 15.

This is urgent and need help.

Regards and thank you.

Arun Ghewarchand

entry.title: Re: Assignment of the type "String" to type "Numeric" typ...
entry.content:
Hi, mahendra_143,

I am going through unanswered questions to try to get a response out to people.  I wonder if you were able to resolve the problem here or perhaps you created a support case to assist you.  If you still need assistance, it might help readers be able to respond if you share detailed steps of what you are doing, where you write the code (share the code) and what is returned on the screen so we might be able to respond to you.

If resolved, feel free to indicate that as well.  (In that case, if you are willing/able, share details of how you solved it in order to help other users in the community who might come across the post.

Best regards,

Paula

entry.title: Watch the SAP Fieldglass 2025.05 Release Highlights Video Series
entry.content:
The SAP Fieldglass 2025.05 Release Highlights videos are now available. These videos showcase exciting self-service innovations for Configuration Manager users, general highlights including exciting AI and Contingent Labor updates that focus on the importance of skills-based hiring, and new Services Procurement enhancements.

  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.05 Release Configuration Manager Highlights
  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.05 Release General Highlights
  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.05 Release Services Procurement Highlights

You can also watch the full series on the SAP Fieldglass Product Success Work Zone where you’ll also find additional release resources such as the Spotlight Features Guide.

entry.title: BYD2502 Details of summary posting in rev rec run
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,


Our Auditors are asking details of Details: General Ledger Account XXXX posting due to revenue recognition run process ...

when we execute revenue recognition run we get Postings tab with Accounts tab summary of each GL Account posting at summary level and details below in detail tab
Details: General Ledger Account XXXX

But this detail is not enough Auditors are asking all posting to P&L GL Account with details like .. who posted the time how many hours which hours posted as been contributed to revenue recognition run and which were delayed due posting block or any other reasons ..
any time booking cleared or expense cleared or any other direct posting ...

how does SAP works out this summary of GL Posting and which time line has been cleared in revenue recognition run and which were not ... could you please tell us details which can help identify such details ..

SAP has any report which has such details .. we tried revenue recognition run history but no luck ..

we need details of P&L GL Posting with full breakdown of how that cost was initially posted by whom etcs.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2502 Details of summary posting in rev rec run
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

In SAP Business ByDesign, the Revenue Recognition Run aggregates and posts revenue and cost recognition entries based on the underlying project time confirmations, expense postings, and other cost objects. The auditors' request goes deeper than the default level of detail provided in the Postings tab of the Revenue Recognition Run — they want traceability from the P&L postings back to the original source transactions (e.g., time recordings, expense reports), including user details, dates, hours, reasons for delay/block, etc.

To meet auditor requirements, you'll need to link several reports like (Project Cost and Revenue Details by Project Structure, Journal, Cost Centers - Line Items, G/L Accounts - Line Items...)

You can get the Revenue Recognition Run ID and date, open Journal report for that period and filter by the G/L Account in question, and For each line item, use Reference Document Type and Source Document ID to trace back to Time Recording Confirmation...

The SAP Business ByDesign does not offer a single standard report that provides:

  • Revenue recognition journal entry

  • Original time/expense document

  • User who posted

  • Delay/blocking reason

  • Posting date and clearing info

However, you can build a custom report using Data Sources. Use Business Analytics > Design Reports to combine these into a joined data source/report.

 

Best regards,  
Ihan
SAP Product Support

entry.title: Re: What's New in SAP BTP – Q1 2025

entry.title: Re: Adding the Shifts to the Resource/Work Center in the Capacities Tab
entry.content:
@Raja_S_Reddy_B 

The document is very helpful, but it does not fully meet my requirement. I have 4 shifts:

  • Day Shift: 8:00 AM to 5:30 PM (Break: 1 hour)

  • Night Shift: 5:00 PM to 3:00 AM (Break: 1 hour)

  • Friday Shift: 8:00 AM to 5:30 PM (Break: 1.5 hours)

  • Ramzan Shift: 7:00 AM to 2:00 PM (No Break)

When I enter only the Day and Night shifts, the system does not show any error. However, when I also enter the Friday and Ramzan shifts, the system displays the following error:
"Shift finish 17:30:00 is after start of next shift 08:00:00 (Message No. CR146)".

entry.title: Re: Tasks allocated in Cloud ALM for a User that is leaving
entry.content:
Hi, Avani_Anand,
I am going through old, unanswered questions and the community and came across your post.  In case you haven't found this information yet or in case others are wondering similar, I am providing the following information:
The guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/add-deactivate-and-delete-users has the following section that details info on 'deactivated' users and their tasks:

Adding, Deactivating, and Deleting Users

To manage users in the user management of SAP Cloud ALM, the identity of the user has to exist in the identity provider.

In SAP Cloud ALM, the Identity Authentication service assumes the role of the identity provider. This means that business users log on to SAP Cloud ALM with the mechanisms and credentials defined in the Identity Authentication tenant. For more information, see Step 1: Onboard Users in Your Identity Authentication Service. From this follows:

  • Before you can add a user to SAP Cloud ALM, the identity of the user has to exist in the identity provider.

  • Deactivating or deleting a user in SAP Cloud ALM does not affect the user in your identity provider.

For the actions below, the status of the user in SAP Cloud ALM is also important. These are displayed in the table column (Table View), on the user card (Card View), and in the user details.

 

 User Status Description

New

The user has never logged into SAP Cloud ALM. If an approval request is pending for this user, then only Waiting for Approval is displayed as status.

Waiting for Approval

There is at least one approval request pending for this user. To process the request, select Approve or Reject in the Approval Request section of the user details (see Processing Approval Requests).

Active

The user has logged into SAP Cloud ALM, is not deactivated, and there are no open approval requests for this user.

Expiring soon

In SAP Cloud ALM, the Identity Authentication service (IAS) assumes the role of an identity provider. When changing IAS settings in the SAP Business Technology Platform, a grace period prevents users from being automatically deactivated in SAP Cloud ALM and therefore losing personal settings and project assignments. The number of days specified in this status indicates the end of the grace period after which the user will be deactivated. A user with this status is not authorized to use SAP Cloud ALM, but after changing the IAS within the grace period personal settings and project assignments will still be valid.

If you do not want to wait until the end of the period, you can deactivate or delete the user as usual.

Deactivated 

The user has been deactivated due to an administrator action. A deactivated user can still log on, but is blocked for all applications. A user administrator can reactivate the user, but the role assignments are not restored.

 

(I removed some detail here.)

Deactivate User 

A deactivated user is blocked for all applications of SAP Cloud ALM. The corresponding user still exists, but is marked as Deactivated. Also, personal settings are deleted and the user is displayed anonymized.

For auditability reasons, you can still correlate the user to the performed actions if you have the role User Administrator or User Viewer.

Note

The user status Deactivated is needed for traceability; it does not make sense for users who have not logged onto SAP Cloud ALM before. Therefore, you can only deactivate users, who have logged into SAP Cloud ALM before, meaning their status was Active).  

-> In case a user has User Viewer or User Administrator role, they will see the involved deactivated user and other users without these roles will only see "Former Member" (see image taken from mentioned document):

I hope this is helpful.

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Unable to create a trial instance in SAP CAL
entry.content:
When trying to deploy the appliance template SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, Fully-Activated Appliance, after clicking the Accept Terms button, the system generates the following error.

Error

We are sorry, we are not able to provide the requested solution at this time. Try again later or contact the SAP Cloud Appliance Library operators.
 
Can you Assist?
 
Thanks

entry.title: Re: CPF for recurring allowance is calculated for full mo...
entry.content: IT0014 entries generally do not split automatically due to changes in IT0186, we can implement custom logic or configuration adjustments to achieve the desired splitting.

entry.title: Re: CPF for recurring allowance is calculated for full month irrespective of IT0186 change
entry.content: IT0014 entries generally do not split automatically due to changes in IT0186, we can implement custom logic or configuration adjustments to achieve the desired splitting

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM - Linked defects
entry.content:
Hi, CristinaVasla,

I am going through unanswered questions on the community and came across your post. 

I am not sure if the following will meet your needs or not, but wanted to share it in case you haven't found a solution for your question.  You can see the inline help offers a suggestion that n the case you have a defect that must be handled by multiple people, you can create a sub-task to accommodate this:

In case this won't help your needs, you can always add your idea to the Influence Campaign:

If you have a request for a feature/function that is not presently available, you can submit it at:

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Operations https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3525

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Custom Trust Configuration - Role Collection Mapping
entry.content:
Great explanation, thanks a lot. I have to move the scenario one step further. My CIS is federated with Okta as Corporate Identity Provider. Okta is not sending any groups or attributes, just providing authentication. I would like to assign groups on CIS so I can map them to role collections on BTP. Unfortunately shadow users are created on BTP only, so users do not exist on CIS. If I create manually user on CIS with the same email as in Okta (and shadow user on BTP) authentication on BTP access fails with errors: " error: unexpected response from authentication service" and "status code: 400, reason: invalid_grant, error description: Invalid authorization code: here_comes_some_code". Is such scenario at all possible to achieve?

TIA

BR

Maciej

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM Defect Statuses and Workflow
entry.content:
Hi,  CristinaVasla,

I am reviewing unanswered questions in the community and trying to get a response to the authors.

There is no standard workflow built into Defects in Cloud ALM. 

If you have a request for a feature/function that is not presently available, you can submit it at:

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Operations https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3525

Best regards,

Paula

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA supervisior dimension older configuration...

entry.title: Re: SAP ME. Can a partner create service requests on beha...
entry.content:
Yes, a partner can create service requests on behalf of a customer in SAP for Me, provided the customer has granted the necessary authorization using the Service Partner User Management application. This allows partners to use their own S-user IDs to create and manage cases on behalf of authorized customers. 

Kindly refer to KBA's below for more information.

Service Partner User Cockpit - SAP for Me
2518736 - Service Partners: How to get authorizations for your S-user ID to create support cases on-behalf of your customers - SAP for Me
2518600 - How to grant support authorization to a service partner S-user using the Service Partner User application - SAP for Me
3575946 - How to use the Copy Authorizations function in SPU Management application – SAP for Me
3594233 - Cloud Administrator is unable to access SPU application due to missing authorization error - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALM Health Monitoring - Filter ABAP.ResponseTim...
entry.content:
Hi, christoph03,

In Health Monitoring > Configuration for an on-premise (ABAP) system, you can set a Monitoring threshold for Response Time:

Regarding setting a task type filter, I am not sure if I get your meaning.  You can pick and choose what Events you want to send an alert for (based on the defined threshold setting, example see the screen shot above).  Here is an image showing several metrics are set to issue an alert, some with email notification, some with ticket creation:

This setup is done in Configuration cog of the relevant system(s).

I hope this is helpful to you.

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: How to remove the left/right margin?
entry.content:
Hi, 

Is there a way to remove the left/right margin so I can display the entire TreeTable?

entry.title: Special handling line item-Ariba Network invoice
entry.content:
Hello,

We are facing issue with commodity code default on Ariba Network invoice when we add Special handling charge.

  • When I add a special handling line on network invoice- it loads in B&I with commodity 78, even though Application.Invoicing.SpecialHandlingCommodityCode parameter has been updated to pick commodity 78121603
  • But If I add a shipping line on network invoice – it loads in B&I correctly with commodity 7810, which matches with Application.Invoicing.ShippingCommodityCode parameter value 7810.
  • Both 7810 and 78121603 have same parent 78.

I asked Ariba support, why there is different behavior for Special handling vs shipping line, see below response.

Based on your initial request, we have updated the commodity code for handling 78121603. We did not make any changes to the Shipping aspect as it follows standard functionality. If we do not alter the commodity code in the parameter system, it will default to a four-digit code. Additionally, if we modify any other standard commodity code, it will attempt to align with the parapet commodity code specifically for the network invoice only.

Does this make sense? Anyone else faced similar issue?

Thanks

Ashish

entry.title: Re: Extract ECC

entry.title: The 26Q report not returning any data after generated successfully from Run Statutory Report spp
entry.content:
After necessary configuration not getting any data after generated the 26Q report.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: The 26Q report not returning any data after generated...
entry.content:
The Withholding Tax (TDS Domestic) Form 26Q enables you to generate Form 26Q required to fulfill the statutory reporting requirements for a business dealing with the domestic vendors in India. You generate this report in the Run Statutory Reports app.

 

Kindly ensure that the TDS challan process is completed by following these steps: post the supplier invoice, create the remittance challan for the supplier, and prepare the required withholding tax return for submission to the tax authorities.

 

Please refer the below help document End-to-End Scenario of TDS amount as an remittance challan,

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/634261119fec4d58970471f2c4a9a740/3a6d6241949f48e6a4d4e1512b2201d7.html?locale=en-US

 

Best regards,

SAP Support - S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: Re: mm/fi integration
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

If you're working as an SAP MM consultant, and you're looking at the MM-FI integration, your main job is to make sure inventory movements and invoice processing in MM trigger the right FI postings that’s where config on the MM side comes in.

Here’s what you actually need to look at:

1. Valuation Area

 

  • Set whether the system does valuation at plant or company code level.
  • Usually done in config early on.
  • Important because it impacts how accounts are picked.

 

2. OBYC - Account Determination

  • This is the big one.
  • You tell SAP which GL accounts to hit when certain things happen — like goods receipt, invoice receipt, or a goods issue.
  • You map transaction keys (like BSX, WRX, GBB) to GL accounts based on:
    • Valuation class of the material
    • Valuation grouping code (tied to the plant/company code)

 

3. Valuation Class / Material Types

  • Material types (e.g., ROH, HALB) control which valuation classes you can use.
  • Valuation class is a driver for account determination — e.g., raw materials hit one account, finished goods another.

 

4. GR/IR Account

  • This is the temporary clearing account used between goods receipt and invoice receipt.
  • Set up under WRX in OBYC.
  • You have to make sure this is maintained for each valuation class.

 

5. Price Control (S or V)

  • Set in the material master (Accounting 1 view).
  • S (Standard price) means any difference goes to a price difference account.
  • V (Moving average) means the system adjusts inventory value with each receipt.

 

6. Tax Codes

  • You configure tax codes in FTXP.
  • They come into play during PO and invoice posting, and they trigger tax-related entries in FI.

 

7. Movement Types

  • These control how the system behaves during goods movements.
  • You can control if they trigger FI postings, which account modifier they use (that affects OBYC), etc.
  • Config is done in OMJJ.

 

8. Vendors (FI Link)

  • Vendors are created in BP with the FI role.
  • You have to assign the correct reconciliation account so when you post invoices, they hit the right line in FI.

 

9. Material Ledger (If Used)

  • If material ledger is active (e.g., for actual costing), it changes how postings work.
  • Not always active, but if it is, it affects price variances and inventory valuation.

 

I hope this will help answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

#GenAI was used to help generate this content.

entry.title: Re: OData Network Error when Using "SAP Build Apps Preview" Android APP (WebApp preview wo
entry.content:
Still looking for a work around. Didn't find any solution.

Stopped working on my project because of this issue. My client want to switch to another platform and stop working with SAP build Apps.

I Use Airtable as a primary database and no problem submitting HTTP Request on it. I only have problems doing HTTP Request to a Service Layer B1 server from SAP Build Apps Preview or Compiled APK.

entry.title: SICF service insert default port
entry.content:

SICF service is picking up http port as default and we would like to change this to https port.
Can you please advice us how this can be achieved?

Regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SICF service insert default port
entry.content:
Hello

To change it, you need to maintain the table HTTPURLLOC as per the steps in the KBA 3288477 - How to maintain table HTTPURLLOC? 

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Unlock Simplified U.S. Tax Compliance with SAP's New Workspace

entry.title: Re: QM Manage Usage Decision - field Scrap/ Reverse UD
entry.content:
Hi @MonikaKeblovska,

for app "Manage Usage Decision" there are no extensibility options provided to check field entries. You could request this on the Customer Influence site.

I would recommend to create a view as default public view with only the most used posting options for stock postings to avoid unintended postings to scrap e.g.: 

Kind regards

Franziska

entry.content: within the transaction DBACOCKPIT you can use the "SQL Editor"

entry.title: Handling Multilingual Role Descriptions in SAP: A Consultant's Guide to SE63
entry.content:
In multinational SAP environments, it's common for business roles to be created in one logon language - say, Russian, German, or French - only to later require translation or renaming into another language like English. This seemingly simple task can become unexpectedly tricky.

Imagine this scenario: a consultant creates a business role with a description in their default logon language. Later, the client asks to update the role name or translate its description for English-speaking users. You log into SAP in English, go to transaction PFCG, open the role… and the description is missing or shows as ############. No matter what you try, the system suggests using transaction SE63 for text translation.

But here’s the catch - SE63 might seem confusing when it comes to renaming or translating role descriptions. The menus are not always intuitive, and without clear guidance, it’s easy to get lost.

In this blog post, I’ll walk you through step-by-step instructions on how to properly translate or rename business role descriptions across languages using SE63. This guide is tailored especially for SAP consultants who want a reliable method that works, without guesswork.

And finally, once the translation or renaming is complete, I’ll also show you how to correctly transport the updated role descriptions through your SAP landscape - from Development (DEV) to Quality (QAS) and eventually to the Production (PRD) system. Because yes - translations also need to be transported.

 

Step-by-Step: Translating SAP Role Descriptions Using SE63

Let’s walk through a real-world example where a role was created in Russian and needs to be translated into English. The steps below apply to any language combination and any system landscape where proper transport is required.

1 Role Created in Russian

We begin with the role Y_MAINT_REPORTS, which was created while logged into SAP in Russian. The role's description is entered in Russian as:

Отчеты ТОРО

 

2️ Switch to English – Description Disappears

Next, we log in to SAP using English as the logon language. We open transaction PFCG and switch the role Y_MAINT_REPORTS into Change mode. Now we see that the description is blank.

Additionally, SAP shows a message below stating:

“You can only maintain role texts if logged on in original language RU. Texts for the role exist in language RU. Use transaction SE63 to translate missing texts.”

3️ Run SE63

We run transaction SE63, but the initial screen is not very intuitive. Many users get stuck here. To proceed, you need to run a specific command depending on your system version:

  • For SAP_BASIS 7.00 SP07 and higher: enter **ACGR** in the transaction command field.
  • For SAP_BASIS 4.6C to 7.00 SP06: enter **AGL**.

In our case, we’ll run ACGR in the command bar.

4️ Enter Role Name and Translation Parameters

After executing ACGR, you’ll be taken to a screen with the following key fields:

  • Object Name: enter the role name (Y_MAINT_REPORTS)
  • Source Language: the original language of the role (e.g., RU)
  • Target Language: the language you want to translate into (e.g., EN)

Click Edit to continue.

5️ Translate the Role Description

In the next screen, you’ll see the existing description (in our case, in Russian) and an empty field below where you can enter the new description in the target language.

We enter the English translation, for example:
Maintenance Reports

Click Save, then click the Back button.

6️ Prepare for Transport

Back on the previous screen, click the Display Transport Object button. This screen provides the transport data we’ll need to manually add to a transport request.

Note the following details:

Transport Object

  • Program ID: R3TR
  • Object Type: ACGR
  • Object Name: Y_MAINT_REPORTS

Language Transport Object

  • Program ID: LANG
  • Object Type: ACGR
  • Object Name: Y_MAINT_REPORTS

7️ Create a Customizing Request in SE01

Now we’ll move to transaction SE01 to create a transport request:

  1. Run SE01 and create a new Customizing Request.
  2. Open the request and double-click on the Customizing Task underneath it.
  3. Click the Change button.
  4. Add the entries exactly as shown in the screenshots using the data from the Display Transport Object step.
  5. Save your changes.

8️ Verify the New Description

You can now open a new SAP session, log in with the target language (English in this case), run PFCG, and open the role Y_MAINT_REPORTS. The new description will now appear as entered.

9️ Transport the Change Across the Landscape

Finally, after verifying the change in DEV, transport your new Customizing Request to QAS and PRD systems as usual. This will ensure the translated role description is visible consistently across the entire SAP landscape.

✅ Summary

Translating or renaming SAP role descriptions across multiple languages is a task that often confuses consultants, especially when SE63 is involved. But with the right approach—starting from ACGR, entering your role, saving the translation, and creating a proper transport - you can make this process predictable and repeatable.

entry.title: Why Branding is Important for SAP Community Events
entry.content:
5 Value Props

1. Creating a Memorable Experience: A well-defined brand, including a unique logo, color scheme, and overall visual style, helps to make an event stand out. When attendees see consistent branding across all event materials, from the website to the on-site signage, it creates a cohesive and professional impression. This makes the event more memorable and increases the likelihood that attendees will return in the future.

2. Contributing to the Growth of the SAP Ecosystem: Ultimately, effective branding for SAP Community events contributes to the overall growth and health of the SAP ecosystem. By creating engaging and memorable experiences, these events attract new members, encourage participation, and foster innovation. This, in turn, benefits SAP, its partners, and its customers.

3. Fosters Community Identity: Strong branding creates a sense of belonging among participants. When people wear your event t-shirt or display your stickers on their laptops, they become ambassadors for your community.

4. Improves Sponsor Attractiveness: Corporate sponsors are more likely to support events with clear, professional branding that aligns with their own marketing standards.

5. Builds Trust and Credibility: Consistent branding signals professionalism and attention to detail. It assures participants that the event is well-organized and worth their time—crucial factors in the busy SAP professional's calendar.

A Case Study: SAP CHOTA Talks

One excellent example of effective community event branding is "SAP CHOTA Talks," a series of concise, impactful presentations by SAP experts organized by the SAP Community Hyderabad. The name "CHOTA" (meaning "small" in Hindi) perfectly captures the event's concept: concentrated knowledge-sharing sessions delivered in 20 minutes or less.

SAP Community Hyderabad: "Learning, Sharing, Networking & Empowering"

The SAP CHOTA Talks initiative operates under the broader umbrella of SAP Community Hyderabad, which has built its identity around four core pillars: "Learning, Sharing, Networking & Empowering." This powerful theme is seamlessly integrated into all SAP Community Hyderabad events, including SAP Inside Tracks , CHOTA Talks, etc., creating a consistent narrative that resonates with participants.

The Brand Elements

The Logo

The Tagline

"Learning, Sharing, Networking & Empowering"

 

The T-shirts

SAP Chota Talks 2025 Q2

SAP Chota Talks 2025 Q1

Outcome:

SAP CHOTA Talks has achieved remarkable success through its branding efforts:

 

Parting Thoughts

In the competitive landscape of SAP community events, strategic branding isn't a luxury—it's a necessity. As demonstrated by SAP CHOTA Talks and the broader SAP Community Hyderabad initiatives, thoughtful brand development can transform simple concepts into recognized and valued community fixtures that embody the principles of "Learning, Sharing, Networking & Empowering."

Remember, effective branding doesn't necessarily require massive budgets—it requires clarity of purpose, consistency in execution, and an authentic connection to your community's needs. By following these principles and taking inspiration from successful examples like SAP Community Hyderabad, your SAP community event can develop a brand identity that stands out, resonates with participants, and endures over time.

SAP CHOTA TALKS Hyderabad 2025 (Q1) - Event Highlights

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mZCaymb7-OY

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pybnULVh32Y

https://community.sap.com/t5/hyderabad-blog-posts/sap-chota-talks-hyderabad-2025-q2-hyderabad-coming-soon/ba-p/14089945

 

 

@Ruthvik_Chowdary  @KatherineKenneally @lauraschmitz

entry.title: MR11 - GRIR open items display issue
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have a weird situation where the MR11 is automatically netting an open IR (credit memo) with a open GR (reversal - with mvmt - 122) and showing the net on the MR11 screen. Below are the screenshots.

I am expecting each open GR and IR to appear separate and be available for separate Account Maintenance posting via MR11. Would anyone know what setting will fix this on the MR11 report.

The system is S4 private cloud 1809 version.

Thanks very much for your time as well any help / suggestion you can provide.

For Example: On the below example (red highlighted), the system automatically netted the 5143.29 (from the GR reversal) and 878.15 (from the credit memo) to show the net amount of 4265.14 on the MR11 screen.

entry.title: Hi, i have same requirements
Re: Fiori App 4988 - Team Member Authorization Status
entry.content:
I saw something like this a while ago in the community.  

Could you maybe check SAP note 2914245.

Regards

Waza

entry.title: Re: FAGLF101 Not Posting Journal Entry – Empty Posting List
entry.content:
I implemented SNOTE: 3496179 - Postings list is empty in FAGLF101 and solved this issue.

Thanks for all.

entry.title: Re: Detecting Handwritten vs. Digital Docs in SAP CAP + DOX
entry.content: Can you explain at what level the information handwritten vs printed text would be most helpful for your application? A the document level or field level?

entry.title: Re: Requirement to Display Two Sales Org Addresses on MSDS
entry.content:
Hello

the SAP SD process is using exactly one sales organisation. Therefore: This triggers then the EHS part. The "handshake" of data is using one sales organisation code only. I have no clue how you would like to make the link to a second sales org.. but as you have stated: any how a customer driven solution. And yes you can go on like that.

But in a nutshll: if you have a clear blue print concept for the link of the both sales org: you can enhance your logic for chaper 1. This is done by many companies.

But there is a risk: Currently you have one released WWI report. This report contains (for SDS) only the parameter symbols. Only these will be expanded in report shipment,

Therefore: you can only change the parameter symbol to collect data to be pinted. As soon as you would need new symbols. i am sorry. ANY SDS must be regenerated (at least in a minor version)

Your idea to use the property tree will have the same effect.

What i can share to you are discussions like: we have one sales organisations but many distribution channels. Can i have different chapter 1 addresses by channel? 

Using 3E WWI template: Answer is no. Using own chapter 1 logic: answer is yes (but you need to do something (refer above regarding the risks)

Can you explain high level your planned logic: if we have sales org X we shold print data as well for Y. How are you planning this link?

Wish you the best

C.B:

PS: if you have used up to now the SAP standard WWI symbol (for address print): it would be a "modification" from SAP point of view (hos might be a risk) to use a different function module to collect and show the data. But from my point of view this is possible.

PPS: Chapter 1 is discussed in many projects i know. The topic is coming up very often. To avoid risks: stick to standard: only if there is a "good / big business value": you should change the logic

Only if you have a "on scratch EHS implementation": you should think about customer solution. TO change in a running system the solution is a risk.

entry.title: Handling Bank Charges During Incoming Payments in SAP FS-CD
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

I have a requirement in SAP FS-CD related to handling bank charges during incoming payments. Here is the scenario:

 

We request the customer to pay 100 USD (i.e., the receivable is 100 USD).

 

The customer makes a payment of 100 USD, but due to bank charges, we receive only 90 USD.

 

The bank charge of 10 USD is not known at the time of invoicing or payment request, but it becomes evident at the time of payment receipt.

 

 

Requirement:

 

1. When an incoming payment is received:

 

If the amount is less than the expected amount (e.g., 90 USD instead of 100 USD), the difference (e.g., 10 USD) should be automatically treated as a bank charge and not as an underpayment.

 

If the incoming payment is more than the expected amount (e.g., 110 USD), then:

 

System should allocate 100 USD against the receivable.

 

10 USD should be considered as either a bank charge recovery or as an overpayment, depending on configuration.

 

 

 

 

 

Goal:

We want to configure the system to automatically post the difference amount (when less than receivable) as bank charges without manual adjustment, during the incoming payment process in FS-CD.

 

Can you please guide me on how to configure this in FS-CD? Is there any standard way to achieve this, or do we need to implement a BAdI or enhancement?

 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: How to update the 3E Regulation Content in EHS？
entry.content:
Hello

both questions have been discussed very often; the situation is still the same

Regarding: 

  1. How to update the 3E Regulation Content？ => if you have a full 3E license you get cookbooks etc. "on mass": all of them describe the process: how to do the update. Just read these (sorry so say MANY documents) 
  2. If the company has not been updated for several years, Would I only update the latest version of the regulatory content, or do I need to update the process version step by step?

The answer to this question is not as easy. The "content" contains many pieces.

e.G. WWI templates, Ruleses, Phrases, and data

If you refer to the "data" update: it is NOT a good idea NOT to consume the updates regularly

what are the consequence to miss something:

Example: Phrases: we need to seperate: you get new translations and you get new phrases (because of some lgal need)

Depending on the success of the company: you could need the new phrase translation if you go to a new market

There are many service companies (just do google research) which are willing to help you indoing the job (if you have lack of knowledge or not enough people). 

C.B.

entry.title: Re: where i can find the SAP note 1146700. All the links...
entry.content:
Hello

Easy: just log on to SAP marketplace and do a research. You will find all OSS notes (even if it is only a consulting one).

C.B.

entry.title: Re: New customer story from Westwood

Hello

looking on the story: i miss details. what proceses are used in Product Compliance ? Even the cloud version of Product Compliance is not final (according to official roadmap of SAP).

It would help to know whci of the big 4 subapplication are really active?

Dangerous Goods part? Labeling part ? Compliance part? SDS Part?

Thanks

C.B.

entry.title: How to create cash flow statement in sap s4 hana public cloud?
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

How to create a cash flow statement in SAP S/4 HANA public cloud?

entry.title: Re: Dangerous Goods - Checks on FO/FB
entry.content:
Hello

not being the expert in SAP TM: but

"but the DG information should not be shared with the LSP or the forwarder."

Data exchange is happening via documents; either printed or send electronical. I would assume by using SAP TM module properly you can define conditions for theses case. If you need to use customer development... no idea.

Buat as DG information is "legally driven": why not sharing the data? it is not confidential data

C.B.

entry.title: Re: Creating an SAC story with members from different Hie...
entry.content: It's not possible to show two different hierarchy in one single table.

entry.title: Re: Which CDSview can provide the deprecated 0PM_MEASUREM...
entry.content:
Hello @rogerio_vsouza 

The standard CDS views I_MeasuringPoint and I_MeasurementDocument are available and can be utilised to replace the obsolete data source 0PM_MEASUREME.

These CDS views are part of the Virtual Data Model (VDM) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, and are designed to provide standardized access to business data. They can be used directly or extended through custom CDS views to meet specific reporting and analytical requirements.

References:

  • SAP Help Portal: Virtual Data Model (VDM)SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Community Blog: Create CDS views in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and consume them as APIs

Let me know if you need further information

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: How to enable cancel and set release button in Segmen...
entry.content:
Hello,

To cancel the release of a segment in transaction WE31, ensure you are in change mode (Shift + F1) and that the segment is not already used in active IDoc types; also, check that your user has the appropriate authorization (S_DEVELOP) to modify released segments

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Rule-Based Custom Tiles
entry.content: My idea was for the message to appear as a pop-up when you enter the system or at least to know if the employee read or opened the tile. Is that possible?

entry.title: Allow change in Notification Type after created
entry.content:
Hi

I want to change the notification type (Breakdown to predictive maintenance ) by Notification change T code IW 22.
After doing the following configuration it is not working .

IMG-->Plant Maintenance and Customer Service-->Maintenance and Service Processing-->Maintenance and Service Notifications-->Notification Creation-->Notification Types-->Allowed Change of Notification Type

Please advise to resolve it.

entry.title: Unexpected EOF at Target when Calling API Proxy for Standard OData Service via SAP API Management
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I’ve created an API proxy in SAP API Management that wraps an existing standard OData service from our SAP S/4HANA system. The proxy was deployed successfully, and I’ve included the required API Key in the apikey header when testing via Postman.

Steps that I have followed:
1. I selected an API from api.sap.com to fetch/retrieve time-sheet data and tested it in POSTMAN and I was getting the data.
2. I created an API proxy using SAP API Management with included policy of APIkey and published the product.
3.But when I tried calling from postman it is giving this error:

{
  "fault": {
    "faultstring": "Unexpected EOF at target",
    "detail": {
      "errorcode": "messaging.adaptors.http.flow.UnexpectedEOFAtTarget"
    }
  }
}

 

I want a solution to this problem!

Regards,
Shashikant Patel

entry.title: Re: Best way to do Extensibility for Digital Signature in...
entry.content:
Dear Carlos,

Thank you for the detailed explanation.

While SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud does not offer native support for Angola, your approach using the extensibility of the DRC eDocument Framework is feasible. You can build a custom communication scenario via the Document Compliance (DRC) extensibility options to integrate a certification engine for digital signing before the accounting document is posted.

The critical step will be inserting the signing logic in the eDocument processing flow, ideally through BAdIs, custom logic, or a side-by-side extension via SAP BTP. Once the document is signed, the process can trigger the accounting posting—similar to the ECC/S/4HANA behavior you described.

Let me know if you need further input.

Best regards,

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Supplier Invoice Number Ranges
entry.content:
Hello @adnanmaqbool ,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, while you cannot directly configure separate number ranges for supplier invoices within the Materials Management (MM) and Procurement modules per company code, you can influence the number ranges for the corresponding accounting documents through the self-service configuration activity (SSCUI) 101702. This allows you to define accounting document number ranges per company code, which can serve as an alternative method to differentiate supplier invoices across different legal entities.

For more detailed guidance on configuring document number ranges, you can refer to the following SAP Knowledge Base Article

SAP KBA 1758121: How to Configure Supplier Invoice Number Range in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

1758121 - How to Configure Supplier Invoice Number Range in Sequence

Thank you and best regards

Chuma

entry.title: Download of hana kafka Connector
entry.content: I need to download the hana kafka connector, version 2.0.030 or higher, but I can't find the download page.

entry.title: Re: How to create cash flow statement in sap s4 hana publ...
entry.content:
Hello @nithin_pranesh 

You can generate a Cash Flow Statement using the Cash Flow Statement – Indirect Method app.

Follow the links below:

Outputting the Cash Flow Statement – Indirect method 

Enterprise Resource Planning Blog Posts by SAP

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Successfactors Time Management – new features in H1 2022
entry.content: If I want to have the allowance value field pre-filled and only visible to employees, is it possible?

entry.title: Batch determination not working for 261 from PM order
entry.content:
Hi SAP Guru,

Batch determination failed for 261 in MB1A for PM Order.

Details:

a.Batch determination procedure assigned to 261

Condition type for the procedure: ZKBN

b. MCB1- info record created with Selection criteria: Rem. Shelf Life < 30 days

c. Reservation from PM order with Movement type 261

d. MMBE/MB5M: there are 2 batches which meet the criteria (Rem. Shelf life < 30 days)

e. MB1A for Goods issue allowing for auto batch determination 

Hit enter and none is picked up.

 

Please kindly help me out or provide me some hint and your expertise.

Thank you so much,

Lydia

entry.title: CAPM Basics - Part 4
entry.content:
Hello Colleagues,

In this blog, we will be learning on how to create a complete hybrid application accommodating all the features which we implemented in our previous 3 blogs.

To get started, we will create a binding to the DB and Auth instances deployed on cloud. Run the below commands:

  • cds bind -2 simple_proj-auth
  • cds bind -2 simple_proj-db

This will create a section in your .cdsrc-private.json file which will implement the xsuaa authentication and hana db instance from cloud while running your application on-premise (hence, the name hybrid approach).

In our previous blog, we had created and updated entries in our hana-db using postman. Now we will be creating the UI layer for our application and implementing app-router feature which will handle the exchanging of token based on roles of the user who is logged in.

First, update the services.cds file with the below code snippet and build and deploy your application once more. Follow the commands discussed in the previous blogs. The added annotations will allow user to create, update and delete records from the DB.

using training as db from '../db/data-model';


@path: 'MyService'
service CatalogService {
    @(restrict: [
        {
            grant: 'READ',
            to   : 'Viewer'
        },
        {to: 'Admin'}
    ])
    entity trainers @(Capabilities: {
        InsertRestrictions: {
            $Type: 'Capabilities.InsertRestrictionsType',
            Insertable
        },
        UpdateRestrictions: {
            $Type: 'Capabilities.UpdateRestrictionsType',
            Updatable
        },
        DeleteRestrictions: {
            $Type: 'Capabilities.DeleteRestrictionsType',
            Deletable
        },
    }) as projection on db.trainer;

    annotate trainers with @odata.draft.enabled;

Once the application is successfully re-deployed, right click on your mta.yaml file and choose to Create MTA Module from Template as shown below.

Select SAP Fiori Generator and click Start.

Choose List Report Page and click on Next.

Select the following options for Data Source and Service Selection and click Next.

Select the following in Entity Selection page and click Next.

Update the Module Name and App Title in Project Attributes page and click Next.

Keep the default options in Deployment Configuration and click Finish.

Notice that all the necessary artifacts related to our web application has been generated in our app folder.

Now since we have implemented XSUAA, we need some mechanism to handle the authorization requests on our behalf. This is where app-router comes into the picture which we will implement in the following steps.

  • Add App Router to the app folder of your project --> cds add approuter
  • Install npm packages for App Router --> npm install --prefix app/router

Notice that a new section is created in your app folder. This will take care of fetching and forwarding the auth token.

 

Its time to run our application now.

Run the following commands:

  • cf login
  • cds watch --profile hybrid
  • The above command will start the application. In another terminal, run cds bind --exec -- npm start --prefix app/router

When you launch the application on port 5000, you get the following error:

From the above URL (the page which results in the above error) copy the redirect_uri section and paste it in the redirect_uris section of xs-security.json file.

Update XSUAA service --> cf update-service simple_proj-auth -c xs-security.json

Relaunch your application. Now you will see default home page being launched successfully. Click on webapp. Your List Report will populate with the data from your HANA DB from cloud.

As per your roles defined, you can created/update/delete records from the DB using the UI. So without writing a single line of UI code, just by defining our services we are able to have a fully fledged CRUD application. This is the beauty of CAP 🙂

Hope you enjoyed reading and following this CAPM Basics series. As always, feel free to leave a comment if you are stuck with anything.

More to follow, so stay tuned!

entry.title: Analytic privileges
entry.content: How to get all the list of Analytic privileges in HANA Studio

entry.title: S/4HANA for HCM - Functions Not available in payroll schema
entry.content:
Hello,
in the notes on S/4HANA for HCM it is described that function P0083 is no longer available in the payroll schema.
if function P0083 is still available, payroll is canceled.
The same happens if function P2005 is still active in schema TC00. Function CHECk ABR is also omitted, but a warning is issued here.
It would be great if the omission of the functions P2005 and CHECK ABR were just as prominent as P0083 in the corresponding note.

Valeska Böger

entry.title: Redis on SAP BTP: When and Where to Use Shard Count Configuration
entry.content:
Modern applications tend to scale to meet growing user demands and real-time expectations, the performance of backend systems becomes a crucial topic when designing architectures. Redis on SAP BTP, is often used for caching, real-time analytics, session management, leaderboard management and many other use cases. When deploying Redis on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), a crucial yet often underestimated configuration is the shard count.

In this post we try to deep dive into why shard count matters, its advantages, and when you should consider tuning it based on your application’s needs.

What is shard count in Redis?

In the context of Redis in cluster mode, sharding refers to splitting data across multiple nodes (or shards). Each shard holds a subset of the keyspace. The shard count determines how many such divisions exist, and it uses hash slot mapping to determine where each key resides.

On SAP BTP, when provisioning Redis in cluster mode, you can configure the number of shards to match your performance and scalability goals. Please be aware the cluster mode is not available in all service plans and hyperscaler/regions, check here for more details.

Why shard count matters

  • Performance through parallelism: higher shard counts allow Redis to distribute workloads across more nodes. This means more parallelism in handling reads and writes, especially under high concurrency scenarios multi-user gaming leaderboards.
  • Improved resource utilization: more shards mean each shard handles less data, enabling better use of CPU and memory, it helps prevent bottlenecks on a single node and improves overall system responsiveness.
  • Horizontal scalability: if you expect your data or traffic to grow, a well-configured shard count provides a scalable path forward, with Redis cluster mode you can handle this by balancing data distribution across the available shards automatically.
  • Fault isolation and resilience: when using multiple shards (with replicas), node-level failures affect only a part of the keyspace, this limits the blast radius of failures and enhances high availability.

Example of use cases where shard count configuration brings value

  • High-throughput applications: applications processing thousands of operations per second — such as gaming leaderboards, session management in chat applications — benefit significantly from increased shard counts.
  • Large dataset caching: for systems caching complex and  large result sets, distributing data across shards prevents memory exhaustion and allows efficient data access.
  • Multi-tenant SaaS platforms: Redis clusters with higher shard counts can isolate data better across tenants, reducing contention and improving response time consistency.

Best practices for shard count configuration

  • Start small, scale carefully: begin with a reasonable number of shards (e.g., 3 or 6) based on expected traffic, and monitor usage and performance.
  • Avoid over-sharding: too many shards can introduce complexity and resource overhead.
  • Ensure even key distribution: Use Redis hash tags or key design best practices to balance data across shards.

Important

Before deploying your Redis on SAP BTP configuration check here the minimum number of cache nodes per shard for the different service plans and the entitlements need.

In summary

Choosing the right shard count in Redis on SAP BTP is a architecture decision. It affects not only performance and scalability but also availability and cost-efficiency. Understanding your application’s data access patterns and workload characteristics will help you make an informed choice and set the correct configuration deployment that supports your business requirements.

 
Thanks for reading!
For more insights from the product team on Redis on SAP BTP, don't miss to read:
  • Redis on SAP BTP - INDEX of official blog posts
Regards, 
Antonio

entry.title: Re: Fiori Push Notification with Situations handling or w...
entry.content:
Hello @Gerd_x 

You aim to trigger a push notification when a specific order type is created, targeting designated users identified via a custom CDS view. The notification should appear on mobile devices or in the Fiori shell bar, with minimal custom development and preferably using standard configuration.

 

Section

Details

Option 1: Situation Handling (Recommended)

Feasible. The Extended Framework for Situation Handling allows for custom scenarios based on business objects. You can:

• Define custom situations triggered by specific order types.

• Determine recipients using a custom CDS view.

• Deliver notifications to users via the Fiori Launchpad or SAP Mobile Start. Refer to the SAP Help Portal for detailed guidance: Situation Handling - Extended Framework.

Option 2: Workflow (SAP Business Workflow)

Possible but not ideal. Implementing this via SAP Business Workflow would require:

• Development of custom workflows.

• Expertise in workflow configuration.This may not be the optimal approach due to the lack of workflow development resources.

Option 3: Other Notification Providers

Limited applicability. While SAP S/4HANA includes various notification providers (e.g., /IWNGW/DEMO), these are typically tied to standard applications. Utilising them for custom scenarios would necessitate development efforts, which contradicts the minimal development requirement.

Let me know if you need further input

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 2020 Free Trial - how to migrate to d...
entry.content:
To find the documentation for migrating to DataDirect 7.1.5, you can refer to the following resources¹ ² ³:
- *DataDirect Documentation*: The DataDirect documentation is available on the Progress website. You can find installation guides, release notes, and user manuals for various DataDirect products, including ODBC connectors.
- *Knowledge Base Article*: Check the Knowledge Base article "Where to find DataDirect product documentation" for detailed information on accessing the documentation.
- *Progress SupportLink*: If you have a SupportLink contract, you can access the documentation and installation guides through the Progress SupportLink portal.
- *DataDirect Connect Series for ODBC Installation Guide*: This guide provides detailed instructions for installing and configuring DataDirect ODBC drivers.
- *Release Notes*: Review the release notes for DataDirect 7.1.5 to understand the changes and enhancements in this version.

To migrate to the new version, follow these general steps:
1. *Download the latest release*: Get the latest version of DataDirect from the Progress website.
2. *Extract the files*: Extract the downloaded files to a temporary directory.
3. *Run the installer*: Run the installation program and follow the prompts to complete the installation.
4. *Configure the drivers*: Configure the ODBC drivers according to your needs, using the documentation as a reference.

If you encounter any issues during the migration process, you can contact Progress Support for assistance.

entry.title: Re: How to remove the left/right margin?
entry.content:
Hi rammel_sapdev,

this could be some padding from the surronding container control. Check if you can apply "sapUiNoContainerPadding" as CSS class to it:
https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/entity/sap.ui.core.ContainerPadding

Maybe have a look at the TreeTable Example, too (this doesn't show any margin for me):
https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/entity/sap.ui.table.TreeTable

Зарегистрируйтесь на Kuc𝐨in с реферrальным кодом QBAYT4XB и получите:

 

  • 50 % пожизненную скидку на торговые комиссии
  • Бонус до 10 800 USDT для новых пользователей
  • Доступ к специальным акциям и предложениям
  • Код действует навсегда, а не только при регистрации

 

 

Kuc𝐨in — одна из самых надёжных мировых криптобирж, предлагающая низкие комиссии, высокую ликвидность и расширенные инструменты для трейдеров любого уровня.

До 10 800 USDT

Промоакции и награды для новых пользователей

 

 

 

 

Как начать

 

 

  1. Перейдите на сайт Kuc𝐨in и создайте аккаунт
  2. Введите код QBAYT4XB при регистрации
  3. Начните торговать и пользуйтесь всеми преимуществами

 

 

Это один из самых выгодных официально доступных кодов с максимальной скидкой на комиссии. Начните с экономии уже сегодня.

Це один з найвигідніших офіційних кодів на платформі. Почніть вже сьогодні та економте з першої угоди.

entry.title: Automatic integration of Change Documents in managed RAP BO not supported on Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi community,

I've used change documents in RAP before, but I needed to handle it within an additional save method. I just realized (must be quite new) that there is now a more automatic integration possible explained here:
https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/integrating-change-documents?locale=en-US

I tried using this in my RAP behaviour definition, but I am getting a syntax error for the following statement:

changedocuments master ( <id_of_the_chdoc_obj> )

It states that "changedocuments" is not supported with the current ABAP release. I am working on a Public Cloud system. I'm wondering on which release this is working (maybe ABAP Platform on BTP?). This would be one of the first things I encounter, which is supported over there but not on public cloud.

Can someone confirm this? It would also be great to know if this will also be available for Public Cloud.

Thanks in advance!
Daniel

entry.title: Can I have multiple dashboards with different models in a Single SAC story?
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I have a doubt, when I am importing a dashboard to a story the dashboards default model gets changed to another model which is already present in the story. When I try to change it to the required one then the other dashboard default model changes as if both can have only one default model.

Please tell me if it's possible as I have multiple dashboards with different models which I want to import in one story and because of this default model problem my input controls are not working.

entry.title: Re: BAPI Extension for Customer added Fields
entry.content:
Hi,

Until filling extension in and passing it to Bapi is fine, but my query is how we need to handle this custom data so as to export them to custom screen placed in PO at Item? what's the placeholder where we could retrieve the extension data and export it to corresponding screen fields?

Let's say: I have recently added a custom tab in PO Item level using exit MM06E005 and Now i need to display the data in the custom fields of Custom tab when PO is Created via Bapi_po_Create1. However, I was able to fill out the extension in with custom fields data using enhancement structure BAPI_TE_POITEM for PO item. However, I am trying to export Custom data from extension to screen fields by mapping them inside the corresponding exit, but not able to export the data to screen despite tried out in multiple ways.  Can anyone help me out with this? Thanks.

entry.title: Software download - technical error
entry.content:
Hi, I was installing software to my new mac device and tried to download necessary software from https://developers.sap.com/trials-downloads.html. I read previous q&a that was related to this technical error in software download and did as told(log out the account and try again) but with no success.

I tried different source link(Transaction load monitoring tool for SAP Business One, ABAP Platform 1909 Developer Edition on SAP HANA 2.0, SAP NetWeaver AS ABAP Developer Edition 7.52 SP04) to see if the error was on specific link, but it showed up in all trials. 

Hoping for fast reply, Nam.
